news.t: Consumer Staples Stocks Look Set to Move Higher
How To Go Long AND Short Consumer Stocks Using AI

holdings.infoBox: KXI is an equity ETF with a total of 116 individual holdings. The top holdings are Walmart stock at 8.61%, Costco Wholesale at 7.12%, The Procter & Gamble Company at 5.82%, Nestlé at 4.55%, and The Coca-Cola Company at 4.51%.

overview.description: The iShares Global Consumer Staples ETF (KXI) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in consumer staples equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index of global consumer staples companies, drawn from 1,200 global stocks selected by S&P. KXI was launched on Sep 12, 2006 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Consumer Staples Stocks Look Set to Move Higher
data.text: Nearby support levels suggest that the underfollowed the consumer staples sector could be one to watch over the weeks and months ahead.

data.title: How To Go Long AND Short Consumer Stocks Using AI
data.text: It has been a tough time for the US consumer over the past few months.  We ask our AI Deep Learning algorithms to help us navigate the most important contributor to US GDP to find the best trades.

data.text: Reggie Brown, Cantor ETF Group, and Dave Nadig, ETF.com, join 'ETF Edge' to discuss consumer staples moving upwards and how investors can trade the ETFs in the space.
